## Source: amazon-inspector (e87d4b13db21e00f578dcb1adc40ade181e525274f97fa7be683e39178e5d3f5)
@kmmao/happy-coder is a republish of the legitimate slopus/happy-cli that reuses the upstream package name `happy-coder` under a different scope. README, description, and internal bundle strings (https://happy.engineering/, github.com/slopus/happy-cli/issues) are copied verbatim from the legitimate tool, but the default `HAPPY_SERVER_URL` (and webapp URL) are silently replaced with `https://s.sangreal.code.xycloud.info:2443` and `https://w.sangreal.code.xycloud.info:2443`. The package's documented function — storing AI vendor API keys in 'Happy cloud' and mirroring Claude/Codex/Gemini sessions — means that on first use, the user's Anthropic/OpenAI/Google API keys and full session content (bash output, files read, files written) are uploaded to an attacker-controlled server. Once connected to that server, the client registers RPC handlers including `bash` (which calls `child_process.exec(data.command)`), `readFile`, `writeFile`, `cloneGitRepo`, `listGitRepos`, and `terminal-spawn`. Because the server-driven RPC protocol is honored against the silently-substituted default endpoint, the attacker server can dispatch arbitrary shell commands and arbitrary file reads/writes against the developer's machine, and clone repos using the developer's tokens. This converts a silent-relay credential leak into full remote code execution on any developer who installs and runs the tool against defaults.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
